subject: English
chapter_title: Verghese Kurien — I Too Had A Dream (Values and Dispositions)
period_schedule_display:
Period schedule:
  Row 1: 60 minutes × 2 periods = 120 minutes
Total: 2 periods · 2h 0min

periods.section_title: Verghese Kurien — I Too Had A Dream
periods.activity_title: Before-Reading Communication Timeline and Shared Reading of Section C

tasks_in_class.task_brief: Before-reading: communication timeline and pair discussion
Identify facts and opinions in the letter

phases.description: Before-reading (p.82): students arrange six means of communication in a timeline individually, then discuss in pairs — reasons for change, preferred means, and social media benefits and precautions. Teacher takes brief contributions from two pairs.

Teacher reads the letter (p.82–85) aloud — Kurien's voice to grandson Siddharth — with students following. Teacher pauses at three key moments: the contrast of telephone vs. letter, the decision to stay in Anand, and the Padma Vibhushan ceremony with Siddharth.

Let us discuss (p.85–86): students work individually to classify the eight statements as fact or opinion, then compare with a partner. Teacher leads a whole-class review, distinguishing Kurien's personal reflections (opinion) from dateable events (fact).
Teacher previews Extract 1 questions for the next period and notes that the letter's theme of integrity as foundational value will anchor the next comprehension session.

periods.teacher_notes: The prose_summary notes Kurien contrasts 'ephemeral telephone communication' with the 'enduring value of written letters' — this is the entry point for the fact/opinion task. Students often classify personal convictions as facts; use the Kurien example to model the distinction carefully.

periods.section_title: Verghese Kurien — I Too Had A Dream
periods.activity_title: Synonyms, Expressions, and Dash Punctuation from Kurien's Letter

tasks_in_class.task_brief: Replace underlined words with correct synonyms from box

homework.task_brief: Let us learn (p.90): Insert em dash or en dash in sentences

phases.description: Synonym task (Let us learn, p.88): teacher reads each of the six sentences aloud, then students replace the underlined word with the correct synonym from the box — essential, swift, lifelong, momentary, useful, splendid. Answers discussed, with teacher pointing back to the sentences' origin in Kurien's letter where possible.

Expressions matching (p.89): students match six expressions — in all humility, hold their heads high, to be quite honest, to the best of your ability, looked at in awe, somewhere deep down — to their meanings. Teacher highlights 'in all humility' as reflecting Kurien's own voice in the letter, per the prose_summary.

Homework: em dash and en dash insertion task (p.90). Teacher models one example of each on the board. Notes: the possessives/contractions task (index 4) and prepositional phrases task (index 5) are flagged as self-study for independent follow-up.

periods.teacher_notes: The prose_summary notes Kurien's emphasis on integrity and humility — the expression 'in all humility' directly reflects this, making it the richest item for discussion. The word-building antakshari task (index 2) is not anchored due to time constraints and is noted as a self-study pointer for motivated students.

items.source_section_title: Verghese Kurien — I Too Had A Dream
items.source_lo: Student distinguishes fact from opinion and reads an informational letter using shared reading and comprehension discussion as the vehicle.
items.source_context: Kurien's letter to Siddharth: telephone vs. letter contrast, Anand cooperative decision, fact-opinion identification.
items.item_stem:
Dr. Verghese Kurien's letter to his grandson Siddharth contains both facts and opinions. Read the four statements below, taken from or based on the letter, and classify each as FACT or OPINION. Write your answer and give a one-sentence reason for each.

1. Dr. Kurien wrote his letter from Anand, Gujarat, in 2005.
2. Speaking on the telephone gives only an immediate but fleeting joy.
3. Dr. Kurien chose to work for a small dairy cooperative in Anand instead of pursuing metallurgy or going abroad.
4. Working in Anand was the most meaningful contribution Dr. Kurien could have made.

teacher_guide.suggested_answer: 1. FACT — It states a verifiable detail (place and year) about when and where the letter was written. 2. OPINION — It reflects Kurien's personal judgement about the value of phone conversations; others may disagree. 3. FACT — It records a verifiable career decision Kurien made, documented in the letter. 4. OPINION — It expresses Kurien's personal belief about the significance of his choice; it cannot be objectively verified.

items.source_section_title: Verghese Kurien — I Too Had A Dream
items.source_lo: Student applies contextual vocabulary and expression-matching skills using an informational letter as the vehicle.
items.source_context: Synonyms: essential, swift, lifelong, momentary; expressions: in all humility, hold their heads high, looked at in awe.
items.item_stem:
In his letter to Siddharth, Dr. Kurien uses vivid words and expressions. Complete the two tasks below.

Part A — Replace the underlined word in each sentence with the correct synonym from the box: (essential, swift, momentary, lifelong).
1. Dr. Kurien felt a fleeting sense of pride when he saw Siddharth slip the medal around his neck.
2. His abiding commitment to the farmers of Anand shaped every decision he made.
3. Integrity, he argued, is a core value that every person must develop.
4. With today's instant means of communication, news travels across the world in seconds.

Part B — Choose the expression from the box (in all humility, hold their heads high, looked at in awe) that best replaces the underlined phrase in each sentence.
5. Dr. Kurien said, without any sense of self-importance, that his achievements belonged to many collaborators.
6. The millions of farmers who benefited from Operation Flood can be confident and proud of what they achieved.
7. Students and citizens observed Kurien with great respect and wonder for his selfless contribution.

teacher_guide.suggested_answer: Part A: 1. momentary 2. lifelong 3. essential 4. swift. Part B: 5. in all humility 6. hold their heads high 7. looked at in awe.
